title: centaurea crnogorica
intro: Centaurea crnogorica, also known as Balkan cornflower, is a perennial plant species native to the Balkan Peninsula, characterized by its bright blue flowers and edible roots. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it effective in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Centaurea crnogorica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Centaurea crnogorica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the roots or flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
